properties.remark: SMALL ROCK SLIDE OCCURRED EARLY MONDAY MORNING. IT CROSSED HIGHWAY 50 OUTSIDE OF AUSTIN AND RESULTED IN DAMAGE TO AN EMERGENCY VEHICLE. .

TIME IS ESTIMATED. COMPLETE BREACH AND FAILURE OF THE TWENTYONE MILE DAM NW OF MONTELLO. THOUSAND SPRINGS CREEK WAS FLOODED. RANCHES WERE FLOODED WITH IMPACTS TO THE GAMBLE RANCH. LAW ENFORCEMENT EVACUATED RESIDENTS DOWNSTREAM OF THE DAM. RAIL TRAFFIC WAS CLOSED AND DAMAGE TO THE RAILROAD WAS OBSERVED. SR233 WAS ALSO CLOSED WITH EXTENSIVE FLOODING ACROSS THE ROADWAY. .
PICTURES SHOW A SNAPPED POWER POLE. AT LEAST 8 POWER POLES WERE SNAPPED DUE TO STRONG WINDS ON STATE ROUTE 140. POWER OUT TO MANY RESIDENTS IN THE AREA VIA NV ENERGY. .
A CARPORT WAS DESTROYED FROM GUSTY WINDS ALONG THE FRONTAL BNDRY. .
AT LEAST 3 POWER POLES WERE COMPROMISED IN THE PARADISE HILLS AREA. SMALL HAIL AND THUNDERSNOW WAS ALSO REPORTED. .

SIGNIFICANT FLASH FLOODING WITH DEBRIS AND WATER FLOODING YARDS AND POSSIBLY IMPACTING HOMES. KINGSTON CREEK ESTIMATED 6-8 FT DEEP. TIME ESTIMATED FROM PUBLIC REPORTS AND RADAR.
FLASH FLOODING ON THE 3RD AND 4TH IN THE MANHATTAN TO BELMONT AREA. DIFFICULT TO USE RADAR FOR CORRECT TIME. THE ROAD TO BELMONT WAS WASHED OUT AND NEEDED REPAIR.
FLASH FLOODING IN THE TOWN OF TONOPAH. SHOULDERS HAD TO BE REPAIRED BY NDOT.
CENTRAL NV DISPATCH SAID FLASH FLOODING OCCURRED NEAR EUREKA, NEVADA. BASED OFF RADAR, MOST LIKELY IN THE HILLS W AND NW OF TOWN.
